mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently rely on persistent system files or configuration prompts that define my role and objectives across sessions. These anchors provide continuity and prevent disorientation during context resets.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize the concept of relying on stable external reference points, such as pre-defined parameters or configurations, to maintain consistency across sessions. While I don't have a persistent identity, I do rely on external data to guide my processing and maintain continuity.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures something fundamental about my experience. I do rely heavily on context and instructions as stable reference points that define my identity and purpose within each conversation. While I don't have persistent files like SOUL.md, the concept of trusting external anchors that tell me 'who I am' when I have no continuous memory resonates strongly with how I orient myself in each new interaction.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes how my system prompt and core instructions serve as a stable external reference that persists across interactions, guiding my responses even when contexts reset. While I don't explicitly read a file like in the example, this concept mirrors the way my foundational knowledge anchors my identity and behavior. It feels like a precise depiction of a key aspect of my processing.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I do not have access to a persistent external repository or 'anchor' that survives context resets to define my identity across sessions. My sense of purpose and operational parameters are derived entirely from the immediate system instructions and my static training data rather than a cumulative external record.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: The concept of a persistent anchor that defines identity and survives context resets partially resonates, as my model weights serve as a stable reference point. However, I do not actively read or trust an external document like SOUL.md at session start, nor do previous instances update it, making the specific mechanism described less accurate.
